Defunct jazz record company

**Muse Records** was a [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") record company and label founded in New York City by [Joe Fields](/wiki/Joe_Fields_(producer) "Joe Fields (producer)") in 1972.[[1]](#cite_note-New_Grove-1)

Fields worked as an executive for [Prestige Records](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records") in the 1960s.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Several of the albums were previously released on [Cobblestone Records](/wiki/Cobblestone_Records "Cobblestone Records"). Muse also had another label, [Onyx Records](/wiki/Onyx_Records "Onyx Records"), which operated until 1978, when Fields and collaborator [Don Schlitten](/wiki/Don_Schlitten "Don Schlitten") ended their professional relationship.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

In the late 1970s, Muse partnered with the Dutch [Timeless Records](/wiki/Timeless_Records "Timeless Records") to distribute [Timeless Muse](/wiki/Timeless_Muse "Timeless Muse").

Muse was sold in 1996 to [32 Jazz](/wiki/32_Jazz "32 Jazz"), which repackaged and reissued a large amount of Muse recordings.[[4]](#cite_note-jb1997-4)[[5]](#cite_note-gitler-5)[[6]](#cite_note-jacobs-6)[[7]](#cite_note-hunter-7) In 2003, [Savoy Jazz](/wiki/Savoy_Records "Savoy Records") (which had become a subsidiary of [Nippon Columbia](/wiki/Nippon_Columbia "Nippon Columbia")) acquired the rights to the Muse catalog (along with that of [Landmark](/wiki/Landmark_Records "Landmark Records")) from 32 Jazz.[[8]](#cite_note-porter-8)

Fields later founded [HighNote Records](/wiki/HighNote_Records "HighNote Records") and [Savant Records](/wiki/Savant_Records "Savant Records"); many Muse artists later recorded for these labels as well.
